title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.articleTitle: The History of the Renaissance Workshop
content.text: During the Renaissance, the 'bottega' or workshop was the (0) heart of artistic production and education. These bustling spaces were (1) by master artists who took on apprentices to help with large-scale commissions. An apprentice's training (2) of basic tasks, such as grinding pigments and preparing wooden panels. As they (3) experience, they were gradually entrusted with more complex parts of a painting, such as backgrounds or drapery. This collaborative environment (4) that every work leaving the studio met the master's high standards. It was also a place where new techniques were (5) and shared among fellow artists. The master-apprentice relationship was (6) to the preservation of artistic traditions, yet it also allowed for individual innovation. Many famous painters, including Leonardo da Vinci, (7) their start in these very workshops. Today, the legacy of the Renaissance bottega continues to influence modern art schools and the concept of mentorship. It (8) as a reminder that great art is rarely a solitary endeavor, but rather the result of a collective effort and the passing of knowledge from one generation to the next within a structured and highly creative community of dedicated individuals and passionate lifelong learners.

example.explanation: “The heart of” is a common metaphorical way to describe the central part of something.

questions.explanation: “Run by” is used to indicate who is in charge of an organization or space.
“Consisted of” is the correct phrasal verb meaning to be made up of.
“Gained experience” is a standard collocation for acquiring knowledge over time.
“Ensured” means to make certain that something happened or was the case.
“Refined” is the best verb for improving a technique or process.
“Fundamental to” is a strong collocation meaning essential for the basis of something.
“Got their start” is a common idiom meaning to begin a career in a particular way.
“Serves as a reminder” is a fixed formal collocation.
